title: Add Variation-of-Information distance option to CFI (non-linear codependence)
source: LdP newer-corpus verification (4 parallel read-only agents over the added 2012-2026 papers + LdP REFERENCE CODE in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/code, 2026-06-03). Conversation: 'verifica la nueva informacion antes de implementar'.
why: B0131 uses correlation distance d=sqrt(0.5(1-rho)) — LdP-exact and a true metric, but it only tames LINEAR substitution. LdP's CFI seminar + 2020 statistical-association seminar recommend information-theoretic distance (Variation of Information, VI) to catch features that are REDUNDANT via NON-LINEAR combinations ('VI can measure linear as well as non-linear codependence'). The macro/alt-data block plausibly harbors non-linear redundancy that corr-distance misses.
value_rationale: Closes the non-linear-substitution gap in CFI; VI is a proven bounded metric. Gate on evidence tier2 has material non-linear codependence.
scope: Implement varInfo (variation of information) with Hacine-Gharbi optimal binning (numBins) per 2020 statistical-association seminar
Offer VI as an optional distance in cluster_features alongside the corr-distance default
Quick diagnostic first: does tier2 show non-linear codependence (VI cluster structure != corr cluster structure)? If not, defer.
recommendation: Adopt as an option after ONC (B0139); prioritize only if the non-linear diagnostic is positive.

history.reason: verification: add VI distance for non-linear codependence in CFI
